rulings.Case Title: NEIL A. GOTEINER VS. INTERNATIONAL SWIMMING LEAGUE, LTD., A SWISS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Neil A. Goteiner'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reimbursement Of Fees And Costs Pursuant To Business And Professions Code Section 6203(C)
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, February 04, 2026, Line 1. PETITIONER NEIL GOTEINER's Motion For Reimbursement Of Fees And Costs Pursuant To Business And Professions Code Section 6203(C). Continued on the court's own motion to be heard on Feburary 10, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: NICOLAS NEUTALL VS. URBAN ALCHEMY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, February 04, 2026, Line 2. COMPLIANCE HEARING. The Compliance Hearing is CONTINUED TO May 6, 2026, AT 9 AM IN THIS DEPARTMENT and PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO SHOW CAUSE WHY HE AND/OR COUNSEL SHOULD NOT BE SANCTIONED IN AN AMOUNTUP TO $1,000 PAYABLE TO THE CLERK OF THIS COURT. On 11/19/2025, the court adopted the following order: "The Compliance Hearing is CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 4, 2026, AT 9 AM IN THIS DEPARTMENT. [P] The court has reviewed the 11/3/2025 Polities Declaration. This declaration does not identify next steps or a plan for final distribution. At least ten court days before the next hearing, Plaintiff must file a declaration or declarations from appropriate parties articulating a plan for or actual final distribution. Courtesy copies must be provided to the court within one court day of filing. In developing and presenting any plan for final distribution, the parties are instructed to advance this matter to final distribution forthwith without the need for many further compliance hearings and without delay to distribution of the funds to the appropriate parties. If a court order is necessary, a properly noticed and support motion must be timely filed and served for hearing on 2/4/2026." Notwithstanding the directives in the tentative ruling and the Local Rules, Plaintiff never lodged a proposed order. In connection with the instant hearing, Plaintiff failed to timely file the declaration ordered by the court. The compliance hearing is continued to 5/6/2026 to allow Plaintiff to timely file a compliant declaration. Any response to the OSC must be filed no later than 10 court days before the hearing date with courtesy copies delivered to this department within one court day of filing. Also on May 6, 2026, the court will hold a hearing on the order to show cause directed at Plaintiff and his counsel why either or both should not be sanctioned up to $1,000 payable to the Clerk of the court for violating the court's 11/19/2025 Order, as well as violating the directives regarding submission of proposed orders. Plaintiff's counsel is ordered to appear at the 5/6/2026 hearing in person. Plaintiff is ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the above text and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for this hearing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: PAUL CAREY JR. VS. HOTEL 242, L.P. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, February 04, 2026, Line 5. 2 - PLAINTIFF PAUL CAREY's MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Jon-Paul Moorehead and Law Offices of Richard W. Meier's Motion to be Relieved as Counsel for Plaintiff Paul Carey Jr. is GRANTED. Good cause appearing, the motion is granted. Plaintiff's protest regarding inadequate service is without merit. On 12/17/2025, Estefani Marquez swore under penalty of perjury in a proper proof of service she served the motion, declaration and proposed order on Plaintiff by overnight mail. Plaintiff's 12/26/2025 Declaration he was never served is not, itself, accompanied by any proof of service. The court credit's Marquez's testimony. In any event, clear is the fact Plaintiff had actual notice of the motion no later than 12/21/2025, the date he prepared his "Formal. Opposition." If Plaintiff had not yet been served, he had plenty of time to follow up. Any failure to do so is on him. No later than 8:30 am on 2/4/2025, Moving Party must lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org an updated proposed order on MC-053 with accurate information inserted, where appropriate. Note the Order will not be effective until Counsel files a Proof of Service of the executed order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. rulings.Case Title: VELOCITY INVESTMENTS, LLC VS. AZUCENA JIMENEZ-MALDONADO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, February 04, 2026, Line 11. PLAINTIFF VELOCITY INVESTMENTS, LLC HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. Before the court is Defendant and Judgment Debtor Azucena Jimenez-Maldonado's claim of exemption to Plaintiff and Judgment Creditor Velocity Investments, LLC's levy on certain earnings. The hearing is continued to February 13, 2026 at 9 am in this department. The levying officer purportedly is the Sacramento County Sheriff's Office ("SCSO"). Plaintiff and Judgment Creditor served Notice of Hearing on Claim of Exemption-noticing the hearing for 2/4/2026-on Defendant and Judgment Debtor by mail from out of state on 1/22/2026, from Chicago, Illinois. Where either the place of mailing or the place of address is outside the State of California but within the United States, the required notice period is increased by 10 calendar days. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b).) Plaintiff and Judgment Creditor's service effectively resulted in three days' notice for the hearing, which implicates due process. Plaintiff and Judgment Creditor's manner of notice and notice of hearing may, too, be the root of the most practical obstacle to adjudication of the claim of exemption and opposition, which is the court is without Defendant and Judgment Debtor's claim of exemption (EJ-160) or their financial statement (EJ-165). It seems likely that SCSO has not had sufficient time to deliver these materials to the court. The court continues the hearing to allow for a greater notice period and to give SCSO sufficient time to deliver the necessary materials to the court. Plaintiff and Judgment Creditor is ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the above text and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for this hearing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: KENDRICK ANDERSON VS. FCA US, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, February 04, 2026, Line 8. 1 - DEFENDANT FCA US, LLC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendants FCA US, LLC and Michael Stead's Auto Depot Hilltop Chrysler Dodge Jeep Ram's Motion to Strike Portions of Plaintiff's Complaint is OFF CALENDAR. Defendants' motion challenges Plaintiff's initial complaint, but Plaintiff has now filed an amended complaint. Thus, the court orders the instant demurer, which challenges a superseded pleading, off calendar. Plaintiff is admonished regarding his meet and confer duties. In connection with demurrers and motions to strike, The Legislature clearly imposed upon both parties a duty to make a reasonable and good faith effort through personal communication to resolve the issues regarding the cross-complaint. (See Code of Civil Procedure sections 430.41 and 435.5.) Not participating meaningfully is not an option and cannot be abided. Here, meaningful participation would have resulted in Plaintiff informing Defendants that he would be filing an amended complaint, sparing Defendants and the court from wasting resources on the demurrer and motion to strike. Moving Parties are ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the above text and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for this hearing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: **Transferred to San Mateo** J.C. CHEYNE & CO., A CALIFORNIA CORP ET AL VS. THE PRODUCE DISTRIBUTION CENTER INC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, February 04, 2026, Line 10. Defendant The Produce Distribution Center Inc.'s unopposed motion to transfer venue is GRANTED. "[T]he court shall, if it appears that the action or proceeding was not commenced in the proper court, order the action or proceeding transferred to the proper court." (Code of Civil Procedure, section 396b(b).) Here, Moving Defendant has demonstrated that Plaintiff did not commence this action in the proper court. The proper court, according to the evidence before the court, is the Superior Court of California for the County of San Mateo ("San Mateo Superior Court"). The court, therefore, orders this action transferred to San Mateo Superior Court. Plaintiff shall pay the applicable costs of transfer. Moving Party is ordered to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the above text and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for this hearing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
